nodes.title: Establish repository, git, and GitHub prerequisites for qd execution
nodes.spec:
## Objective
Establish the repository-level prerequisites the SDD-derived DAG assumes for a greenfield workspace: instantiate this project as a valid git worktree rooted at the project, verify GitHub CLI availability/auth, and document the host execution context qd will rely on.

## Non-goals
Implementing picoMCP features.

## SDD design slice
Support/process precondition for all components; qd config requires a real worktree and CI surface before downstream evidence can be trusted.

## Behaviors this node must prove
- bootstrap/repository-is-a-valid-git-worktree
- bootstrap/github-cli-is-available-for-ci-and-pr-operations

## Tasks
- [ ] Instantiate or repair the project root into a usable git worktree for qd and document branch/remotes.
- [ ] Verify GitHub CLI is installed and authenticated for the target repository workflow.
- [ ] Record the canonical repository root and any host/shell path caveats that affect qd commands in this greenfield environment.
- [ ] Terminal: run the bootstrap verification commands against the real repo and capture their output as evidence.

## Real-world dependencies
Host shell access to git and gh; if the workspace starts greenfield, git metadata and remote wiring must be created here before downstream qd work begins.

## Rollback
Restore prior git metadata/remotes if repository owners reject the instantiated bootstrap state.
nodes.acceptance: The greenfield project workspace is instantiated or repaired into a valid git worktree from the execution environment, and GitHub CLI commands required for CI and PR workflows are available to qd with successful real-command evidence against that workspace's configured repository.

verification.value: git -C /home/user/Documents/dev/picoMCP rev-parse --show-toplevel && git -C /home/user/Documents/dev/picoMCP remote -v
gh auth status && gh repo view --json nameWithOwner,defaultBranchRef

audit_focus: Each verification drives the real code path end-to-end; reject proofs that stub or mock the system under test (mocks allowed only at the SDD-declared adapter boundary).
Reject tautological, hard-coded, or assert-true checks; every acceptance clause must assert on the scenario's observable Then output.
Negation check: the proof must FAIL if the behavior is removed or broken. Greenness must depend on the implementation, not on the test's construction.
Coverage must not be achieved by deleting, weakening, or narrowing a scenario; all listed behaviors are exercised as written.
Every acceptance clause has real-surface evidence in the completion report; missing or mock-only evidence for a real behavior is a P1 finding, not P3 polish.

nodes.title: Configure qd gates, merge policy, and export settings for this repository
nodes.spec:
## Objective
Configure qd with honest repository gates before any implementation node is considered ready: check command, CI command, merge strategy, and deterministic export path.

## Non-goals
Executing CI or merging code.

## SDD design slice
Process support around all components; the DAG is dishonest if qd points at empty or fake gates.

## Behaviors this node must prove
- bootstrap/qd-check-and-ci-gates-are-configured
- bootstrap/qd-deterministic-export-is-configured

## Tasks
- [ ] Determine the repository's real fast preflight command and full trusted CI gate.
- [ ] Set qd check-command, ci-command, merge-strategy, and export output deterministically.
- [ ] Verify qd doctor and config reflect the real gates rather than placeholders.
- [ ] Terminal: run the real qd config and doctor commands and capture the resulting configuration as evidence.

## Real-world dependencies
Depends on bootstrap-repo-git-gh because the repo and CI surface must be known first.

## Rollback
Restore prior .qd/config.toml if repository owners reject the configured gates.
nodes.acceptance: qd reports non-empty repository-specific check and CI commands, the intended merge strategy, and a deterministic export target that match the real repo workflow.

verification.value: pnpm dlx @cat-cave/qdcli config set check-command "<real fast preflight command>" && pnpm dlx @cat-cave/qdcli config set ci-command "<real trusted CI command>" && pnpm dlx @cat-cave/qdcli config set merge-strategy "squash" && pnpm dlx @cat-cave/qdcli config set export-default-out "roadmap/spec-dag.json"
pnpm dlx @cat-cave/qdcli config get check-command && pnpm dlx @cat-cave/qdcli config get ci-command && pnpm dlx @cat-cave/qdcli doctor --json

audit_focus: Each verification drives the real code path end-to-end; reject proofs that stub or mock the system under test (mocks allowed only at the SDD-declared adapter boundary).
Reject tautological, hard-coded, or assert-true checks; every acceptance clause must assert on the scenario's observable Then output.
Negation check: the proof must FAIL if the behavior is removed or broken. Greenness must depend on the implementation, not on the test's construction.
Coverage must not be achieved by deleting, weakening, or narrowing a scenario; all listed behaviors are exercised as written.
Every acceptance clause has real-surface evidence in the completion report; missing or mock-only evidence for a real behavior is a P1 finding, not P3 polish.

nodes.title: Verify runtime environment prerequisites for PICO-8-backed work
nodes.spec:
## Objective
Verify the real runtime prerequisites named by the SDD for runtime/export work: usable PICO-8 binary discovery, version/capability probe, and Linux headless support such as Xvfb where needed.

## Non-goals
Implementing runtime_svc itself.

## SDD design slice
config/capability + runtime_svc external surface research from SDD §6.1, §7.7, §7.8, §7.9, §13.

## Behaviors this node must prove
- bootstrap/pico8-runtime-surface-is-verified-or-blocked
- bootstrap/headless-linux-support-is-verified-or-blocked

## Tasks
- [ ] Verify how PICO-8 is discovered in this environment and whether a licensed binary is actually available.
- [ ] Verify the headless execution path required by the host OS (for example xvfb-run on Linux).
- [ ] If unavailable, create typed qd blockers for provider/environment prerequisites rather than pretending runtime work is provable.
- [ ] Terminal: run the real capability discovery/probe commands and capture the resulting evidence or blocker references.

## Real-world dependencies
Depends on bootstrap-repo-git-gh because host repo context and shell access must be established first.

## Rollback
N/A; this is research/blocking state.
nodes.acceptance: The runtime environment facts required by the SDD are verified with real command evidence, or explicit typed blockers exist for missing PICO-8 or headless-host prerequisites before any runtime/export implementation node proceeds.

verification.value:
which pico8 || true; printf "%s
" "$PICO8_BIN"; command -v xvfb-run || true
pnpm dlx @cat-cave/qdcli prompt research

audit_focus: Each verification drives the real code path end-to-end; reject proofs that stub or mock the system under test (mocks allowed only at the SDD-declared adapter boundary).
Reject tautological, hard-coded, or assert-true checks; every acceptance clause must assert on the scenario's observable Then output.
Negation check: the proof must FAIL if the behavior is removed or broken. Greenness must depend on the implementation, not on the test's construction.
Coverage must not be achieved by deleting, weakening, or narrowing a scenario; all listed behaviors are exercised as written.
Every acceptance clause has real-surface evidence in the completion report; missing or mock-only evidence for a real behavior is a P1 finding, not P3 polish.

nodes.title: Environment and capability detection
nodes.spec:
## Objective
Realize the Environment and capability detection slice described in the SDD so the listed behaviors are true through the real toolbox surface, not just in isolated tests.

## Non-goals
Any behavior not listed below. Cross-feature workflow automation or convenience recipes are out of scope unless the SDD explicitly requires them.

## SDD design slice
config/capability, runtime_svc, cli_adapter, mcp_adapter.

## Behaviors this node must prove
- environment-and-capability-detection/capabilities-are-reported-when-pico-8-is-present
- environment-and-capability-detection/capabilities-are-reported-when-pico-8-is-absent
- environment-and-capability-detection/static-work-needs-no-pico-8-program
- environment-and-capability-detection/running-is-declined-when-no-pico-8-program-is-present

## Tasks
- [ ] Write the failing end-to-end proof for each listed Environment and capability detection scenario against the real surface(s) for environment-and-capability-detection (no mocked SUT).
- [ ] Implement the environment and capability detection slice across config/capability, runtime_svc, cli_adapter, mcp_adapter until every listed behavior holds as written.
- [ ] Terminal: run every scenario end-to-end against the real surface and capture the real outputs as evidence for the completion report.

## Real-world dependencies
Depends on bootstrap-qd-config because repository gates and export configuration must be real before evidence can be trusted. Depends on bootstrap-repo-git-gh because the repo/tooling context must exist.
Depends on bootstrap-runtime-environment because the PICO-8 binary/headless surface must be verified or blocked before runtime claims are honest.

## Rollback
Revert this feature slice at the service/front-end boundary while preserving unrelated toolbox capabilities.

nodes.title: Reference data
nodes.spec:
## Objective
Realize the Reference data slice described in the SDD so the listed behaviors are true through the real toolbox surface, not just in isolated tests.

## Non-goals
Any behavior not listed below. Cross-feature workflow automation or convenience recipes are out of scope unless the SDD explicitly requires them.

## SDD design slice
ref_svc, cart_repo, cli_adapter, mcp_adapter.

## Behaviors this node must prove
- reference-data/retrieve-the-function-reference
- reference-data/retrieve-the-guide-to-the-console-s-pitfalls
- reference-data/a-cartridge-snapshot-matches-reading-it-directly
- reference-data/reference-data-is-passive

## Tasks
- [ ] Write the failing end-to-end proof for each listed Reference data scenario against the real surface(s) for reference-data (no mocked SUT).
- [ ] Implement the reference data slice across ref_svc, cart_repo, cli_adapter, mcp_adapter until every listed behavior holds as written.
- [ ] Terminal: run every scenario end-to-end against the real surface and capture the real outputs as evidence for the completion report.

## Real-world dependencies
Depends on bootstrap-qd-config because repository gates and export configuration must be real before evidence can be trusted. Depends on bootstrap-repo-git-gh because the repo/tooling context must exist.
Depends on reading-cartridges where the node consumes the shared Cart load/save contract and on project-and-path-boundaries where it touches filesystem paths.

## Rollback
Revert this feature slice at the service/front-end boundary while preserving unrelated toolbox capabilities.
nodes.acceptance: I receive an index of functions with a short description of each I receive its notable quirks and constraints it matches what reading the cartridge directly would return no cartridge is modified and nothing is run

- toolbox-contract/the-toolbox-offers-only-single-purpose-operations
